Japanese: ‥‥はあ‥‥　はあ‥‥
‥‥くそっ！ なんでぼくがこんな目に‥‥
English: Grr!! How did I get into this mess...?

Japanese: ‥‥そこまでだ！‥‥
English: That's far enough!

Japanese: ‥‥もう、逃がしませぬぞ‥‥ 成歩堂　龍一ッ！
English: You can't run forever, Mr. Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: い‥‥いったい、 ぼくが何をしたと言うんですか！
English: Wha...!? What have I done wrong!?

Japanese: ‥‥あなたを生かして おくわけには行かない‥‥
English: I cannot allow you to go on like this!

Japanese: で、でもぼくは、 ただのしがない弁護士ですよ‥‥？
English: B-But I'm just a simple defense attorney!

Japanese: 問答無用ッ！
English: Silence!

Japanese: ‥あなたを弁護士とは認めません‥
English: You are no longer worthy of your title.

Japanese: ９月　８日　午前　９時　８分 地方裁判所　被告人第１控え室
English: September 8, 9:08 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 1

Japanese: ‥‥ひ、ひどいユメだった‥‥
English: What a nightmare...

Japanese: もしかして、 この着信メロディーのせいか？
English: And I bet it was this ringtone that caused it...

Japanese: 開廷前に、イネムリなんて するもんじゃないな‥‥。
English: I really shouldn't be dozing off right before a trial starts anyway...

Japanese: あれ。 ‥‥切れちまった‥‥か。
English: Huh... looks like they hung up.

Japanese: ‥‥やれやれ‥‥ やっと見つけたよ‥‥。
English: Ah, good. I finally found it.

Japanese: ‥‥危ないところだったぜ。
English: Talk about a close call. I hate to do this to you, but...

Japanese: 悪く思わないでくれたまえよ‥‥ センセイ。
English: It's nothing personal... Mr. Attorney.

Japanese: 同日　数分後 地方裁判所　被告人第１控え室
English: A few minutes later... District Court Defendant Lobby No. 1

Japanese: アタマが‥‥ズキズキする‥‥。
English: Ouch... My head... It's throbbing...

Japanese: それに、なんか‥‥ カスミがかかったような‥‥
English: And why does it feel... so foggy in there...?

Japanese: おはよーございますッ！
English: Gooood morning!

Japanese: うわっ！
English: Ack!

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥。 おはようございます‥‥。
English: Uh... G-Good morning...

Japanese: どうしたんですかッ！ 元気ないですよッ！
English: What's wrong!? You don't look well!

Japanese: ニンゲン、朝一番がカンジンです！ ホラ、ファイトファイト！
English: People are at their best first thing in the morning! Where's that fighting spirit!?

Japanese: ‥‥うう、すまないけど ドナらないでくれるかな。
English: Sorry, but can you please turn the cheeriness down?

Japanese: なんか‥‥、アタマいたくて。
English: My head... sort of hurts...

Japanese: はッ！　了解しましたッ！
English: Roger that!

Japanese: ‥‥あの。何か‥‥？
English: Um... Am I in trouble or something?

Japanese: は？　何か、と言いますと？
English: Huh? "Trouble...?"

Japanese: い、いやいや。 きみ、婦警さんでしょう？
English: W-Wait, never mind. You're a policewoman, right?

Japanese: 何かぼく、悪いコトでも しちゃったのかな、って‥‥。
English: I thought maybe I had done something wrong...?

Japanese: なな、ナニ言ってるんですかッ！
English: Wh-What are you talking about?

Japanese: ごヤッカイになるのは、 アタシの方ですよッ！
English: I'm the one in trouble!
What?

Japanese: 今日はスズキ　マコ、成歩堂さんに この命を預けますからねッ！
English: I'm placing my life in your hands today, Mr. Phoenix Wright!

Japanese: い‥‥イノチ‥‥？
English: Life... in... my hands...?

Japanese: ヤクソクしてくれましたよね！ かならず無罪にしてくれるって！
English: You promised me! You said you would prove that I was not guilty!

Japanese: む‥‥ムザイ‥‥？
English: "N... Not... guilty"?

Japanese: あらゆる弁護士に見捨てられて、 もーダメ、って思ったそのとき！
English: Just when I thought all hope was lost; when all the other lawyers had laughed me off...

Japanese: 『ぼくにまかせろ！』のヒトコト。 ‥‥あの、成歩堂　龍一がッスよ！
English: "Leave it to me!" you said! You! The one and only Phoenix Wright came to save the day!

Japanese: めちゃカンドーしたッス！ ナミダしたッス！
English: And just like that, I was moved to tears, sir!

Japanese: スズキ、一生忘れないッス！
English: I'll never forget what you're doing for me, EVER!

Japanese: （ナニを言ってるんだこの子‥‥）
English: (What is this girl babbling about...?)

Japanese: じつはスズキ、法廷マニアッス。 特に成歩堂さんの大ファンッス。
English: Actually, I really love to watch court proceedings, and I always root for you to win!

Japanese: 非番のときは、よく傍聴に‥‥
English: When I'm off duty, I like to come here and...

Japanese: どーしたんですか、さっきから？ アタシのカオばっかり見て。
English: What's wrong? You've been acting really strange and you keep staring at me.

Japanese: スズキ、ちょっぴりテレるッス。
English: You're making me kind of nervous, sir...

Japanese: あ、ご‥‥ごめん。
English: Oh... sorry.

Japanese: （うーん‥‥これを尋ねると、 エラいコトになりそうだけど）
English: (Hmm... I'm afraid to ask, but here goes...)

Japanese: あの‥‥悪いんだけどさ‥‥。 どなたでしたっけ？　きみ。
English: So, this might sound bad, but... uh... Who are you...?

Japanese: ええええええッ！！
English: Whaaaaat!?

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！　それはないです！
English: Mr. Wright!! How can you say that!?

Japanese: 被告席に立とうという女の子に、 そんなジョークかますなんて‥‥
English: How can you do this to the fragile heart of a girl about to go on trial...?

Japanese: ‥‥ヒドいッ！
English: You're absolutely horrible!

Japanese: いや、あの。
English: No -- I mean, I didn't mean it like that!

Japanese: それが弁護士のするコトですかッ！ スズキ、信じられないッス！
English: Is this how a defense attorney treats his clients, sir!? I can't believe this!!

Japanese: いや。だから。 ‥‥人ちがいだよ、弁護士なんて。
English: No, it's just... ...Well, I think you have the wrong person.

Japanese: だってぼくは‥‥
“ぼくは”‥‥なんですか？ "I'm..."!?
‥‥ぼくは‥‥‥‥ダレだ？ （な‥‥ナニも思い出せない‥‥）
English: ... I'm... Who am I? (Why am I drawing a blank...?)

Japanese: そろそろ開廷時間です。
English: The trial will begin shortly.

Japanese: 被告人と弁護人は、 すぐ法廷に入りなさい！
English: Will the defendant and her lawyer please proceed to the courtroom immediately!

Japanese: ホラ！　もう時間ですよ！ よろしくおねがいしますねッ！
English: The trial's about to start! I'm counting on you in there, OK?

Japanese: （‥‥これってやっぱり “記憶喪失”ってヤツだよな‥‥）
English: (Hmm... I guess I must have amnesia...)

Japanese: （とにかく、これまでの話を まとめてみよう‥‥）
English: (Let's see... What can I piece together...?)

Japanese: （とりあえず‥‥ どうやらぼくは、弁護士らしい）
English: (Hmm, from our conversation, I can safely say that I'm probably a defense attorney.)

Japanese: （そして、 この子を無罪にするなんて‥‥）
English: (And that girl... I said I'd prove her "not guilty"...)

Japanese: （そんなムセキニンな 約束をしてしまっているようだ）
English: (I can't believe I made such an irresponsible promise.)

Japanese: ‥‥だれか、教えてくれ！
English: Aaaaaargh! Someone, please!!

Japanese: コレはいったい、 なんの冗談なんだ！
English: Tell me this is just a bad dream!

Japanese: （しかしそれは、あらゆる意味で なんの冗談でもなかった‥‥）
English: (Why do I get the feeling this is one dream I won't be waking up from...? *gulp*)

Japanese: 同日　午前１０時 地方裁判所　第２法廷
English: September 8, 10:00 AM District Court Courtroom No. 2

Japanese: では、須々木（すずき）　マコ の法廷を開廷します。
English: Court is now in session for the trial of Maggey Byrde.

Japanese: 検察側、 準備完了しております。
English: The prosecution is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: どうしましたか？　弁護人。
English: What is it, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: え？　あ‥‥。 もしかして、ぼくですか‥‥？
English: Um, er... Are you talking to me...?

Japanese: 他に弁護人がいますか？
English: Do you see any other defense attorneys here?

Japanese: （‥‥いないのかなあ、 やっぱり‥‥）
English: (I guess not. Urk.)

Japanese: で、どうなんですか？ できていますか、準備の方は？
English: Now then, are you ready?

Japanese: （とりあえず『はい』って 言っておくか‥‥）
English: (I guess I should say, "Yes" for now.)

Japanese: できていますね？
English: Are you ready, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: はい！ （‥‥って、待てよ‥‥）
English: Yes, Your Honor. (... Wait a sec...)

Japanese: （彼女、“イノチを預ける”とか 言ってたよな‥‥）
English: (If her life is in my hands...)

Japanese: （イイカゲンなことは 言えないぞ‥‥）
English: (I should really do the responsible thing...)

Japanese: あの‥‥。
English: Um...

Japanese: もし“できていない”って言えば、 これでお開きになるんですか？
English: What if I said, "No"? Would that be alright?

Japanese: なるわけないでしょう。
English: Of course it wouldn't!

Japanese: （じゃあ、最初から聞くなよ！）
English: (Then why bother asking to begin with??)

Japanese: すみませんけど、 じつはぼく、キオクがですね‥‥
English: Actually, you see, Your Honor... My memory is kind of...

Japanese: 弁護人のつごうは聞きません。
English: The court will not hear the defense's excuses.

Japanese: 被告が婦人警官ということで、 この事件は注目されています。
English: Because the defendant is a member of the police, this case is under great scrutiny.

Japanese: したがって、ジンソクに 解決しなければならない。
English: Therefore, we must make this trial fair but swift.

Japanese: 前もって、そう言ったでしょう。 忘れたとは言わせませんよ！
English: I believe I have told you this before. I hope you're not telling me you've forgotten!

Japanese: （忘れたと言わせてくれぇ‥‥）
English: (Actually, I did...)

Japanese: では、亜内検事。 冒頭弁論をおねがいします。
English: Mr. Payne, your opening statement, please.

Japanese: はっ。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: ごぞんじのように、被告人は 恋人を殺害した罪に問われている。
English: As I'm sure you're well aware, the defendant is accused of killing her lover.

Japanese: しかもその恋人とは、あろうことか 自分と同じ警察官だったのです！
English: What's worse, her lover was a fellow police officer!

Japanese: け、警官を‥‥。 き、きみ、そんなヒドいコトを！
English: A policeman? You did WHAT to a policeman!?

Japanese: アタシじゃないですよッ！
English: It wasn't me!

Japanese: それに、その。 ベツに町尾さんとは‥‥
English: And besides, Dustin and I...

Japanese: コイビトとか、そんなんじゃ なかったッス。
English: We weren't "lovers" like that!

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく。
English: In any case...

Japanese: 検察側は、被告の犯行を カンペキに立証するでしょう！
English: The prosecution will prove that the guilty party is none other than the defendant!

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: ではさっそく、最初の証人を 呼んでもらいましょう。
English: Mr. Payne, please call your first witness.

Japanese: ふっふっふっ‥‥。 ひさしぶりですね、成歩堂くん。
English: Hee, hee, hee. It's been a while, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: どれだけ成長したか、 私が見てあげましょう。
English: Let's see what you've learned since last time.

Japanese: ムネを借りるつもりで かかってきなさい！
English: I won't show you any mercy this time, rookie!

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。 （ダレなんだよアンタも！）
English: Okaaay... (And who are you again!?)

Japanese: では、イトノコギリ刑事を 入廷させてください。
English: Please bring Detective Dick Gumshoe to the stand.

Japanese: さあ、おねがいしますねッ、 成歩堂さん！
English: Here we go! Don't let me down, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: （逃げ場なし。 カクゴを決めるか‥‥！）
English: (Nowhere to hide... I'm sooo dead...)

Japanese: 証人。名前と職業を。
English: Witness, please state your name and occupation.

Japanese: 自分は、糸鋸　圭介 （いとのこぎりけいすけ）ッス。
English: My name is Dick Gumshoe, sir.

Japanese: 所轄署の、殺人事件捜査担当の 刑事ッス。
English: I'm the detective in charge of homicides down at the precinct, sir.

Japanese: 元気がありませんね、証人。
English: You don't look very well, Detective.

Japanese: その‥‥。被告のマコ巡査は、 自分の部下だったッスから‥‥。
English: Well, sir, the defendant... She works under me, so, you know...

Japanese: ブカだったの？ ‥‥あの刑事さんの。
English: You work under that detective?

Japanese: はッ！　研修時代に、 めんどうを見てもらったッス！
English: Yes, sir! And while I was a trainee, he was always watching out for me, sir!

Japanese: スズキ、一生忘れないッス！ スバラシイ刑事（デカ）ッス！
English: He's such a wonderful guy, sir! I'll never forget what he's done for me!

Japanese: （‥‥どうやら、しゃべりかたに 教育の成果が見られるな）
English: (OK, calm down, I believe you.)

Japanese: では、刑事。
English: Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: まず、かるく事件の 説明からおねがいしましょうか。
English: Please describe for us the details of this murder.

Japanese: 了解ッス。
English: Yes, sir.

Japanese: 事件は、ワレワレの署の近くの 《わんぱく公園》で起こったッス。
English: It happened at the park near headquarters, "Exposé Park".

Japanese: 被害者は、派出所の警察官、 町尾　守（まちおまもる）。
English: The victim was one of the local cops, Dustin Prince.

Japanese: おそらく、上のベンチのあたりから 突き落とされたッス。
English: He was pushed down from the benches on the upper path, sir.

Japanese: 全身を強く打って、 クビのホネが折れていたッス。
English: The landing beat his body up bad and snapped his neck.

Japanese: くわしいことは、きのう みなさんに提出した報告書に‥‥。
English: The details are listed in the report that was distributed yesterday...

Japanese: ‥‥ああ、そうですね。 この解剖記録ですか。
English: Ah, yes. This autopsy report, correct?

Japanese: （‥‥もらったおぼえ、 ないんだけど‥‥）
English: (Why do I not remember getting a copy...?)

Japanese: この報告書によると、
English: I see everything is in order here.

Japanese: 死亡推定時刻が、イヤに ハッキリわかっていますね。
English: Even the estimated time of death is unusually well documented!

Japanese: 被害者の腕時計が、転落の際に 止まっていたッス。
English: The victim's watch stopped from the impact of the landing, sir.

Japanese: 解剖の結果とも一致したので ビシッと時間がわかったッス。
English: The results of the autopsy confirmed the time of death.

Japanese: では、裁判長。
English: If I may, Your Honor,

Japanese: 本日は、この現場写真を 提出いたします。
English: the prosecution would like to submit this photograph.

Japanese: わかりました。 受理しましょう。
English: Very well. The court accepts it into evidence.

Japanese: 証拠品《現場写真・１》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Crime Photo 1 added to the Court Record.

Japanese: それから‥‥きのうの 予備審問の際に、
English: Now then, I recall at yesterday's preliminary hearing,

Japanese: かなり重要な証拠品について、 お話がありましたね。
English: a very important piece of evidence was brought to our attention.

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: そッス。
English: Yes, sir.

Japanese: そうでしたっけ。
English: Yes... I guess?

Japanese: なんですかッ！ あんな重要なコト、忘れたと？
English: Mr. Wright! Is your head on right today!?

Japanese: 死体の下から、重要な 証拠品が見つかったでしょう！
English: There was a very crucial piece of evidence found under the victim's body!

Japanese: そうなの？
English: Um, was there?

Japanese: な、ナニ言ってるんですかッ、 成歩堂さん！
English: Have you lost your mind!?

Japanese: それがその‥‥。
English: Well, actually...

Japanese: キ、キンチョーしてるせいか、 ちょっと忘れちゃって。
English: Um, it's just nerves. Give me a second.

Japanese: ええええッ！
English: Whaaaat!?

Japanese: そんな、シロートさん みたいなコト、言っちゃダメッス！
English: How can you talk like such an amateur!? I thought you were a pro, sir!

Japanese: 了解ッス！ このスズキ、ひとハダ脱ぐッス！
English: Alright, sir. I'll help you through this!

Japanese: そういうときは、法廷記録を 見るといいと思いますよ！
English: At a time like this, maybe you ought to take a glance at the Court Record!

Japanese: ‥‥ほうていきろく？
English: ...Court Record?

Japanese: そッス！　証拠品や関係者の データが、ゼンブそろってるッス！
English: Yup! Info about evidence and people involved with this case are all listed there, sir!

Japanese: Ｒボタンで、いつでも 見られますからね！
English: You can look at the Court Record by touching the Court Record Button!

Japanese: ‥‥Ｒボタン。 くわしいね、きみ。
English: The Court Record Button...? You really know what you're talking about, huh?

Japanese: これでも、婦警さんッス！ そしてさらに法廷マニアッスから。
English: It's too bad I'm a cop, right? Just think! I could totally be a legal aide instead!

Japanese: 弁護人。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: は、はい！
English: Yes, Your Honor!

Japanese: 審理中です。 おしゃべりしない！
English: Court is in session. Save your chit-chat for later!

Japanese: す、すみません‥‥。
English: S-Sorry, Your Honor...

Japanese: （とにかく、法廷記録はチェック しておいたほうがいいな‥‥）
English: (Well, I guess I'd better check the Court Record, and see what I can find...)

Japanese: （Ｒボタン‥‥だっけ？）
English: (What was it again? The Court Record Button...?)

Japanese: 念のため、弁護人に 聞いておきましょう。
English: Alright, Mr. Wright. Let's see if your notes are in order.

Japanese: 被害者の死体の下から発見された 証拠とは、なんですかな？
English: What was the piece of evidence found underneath the victim's body?

Japanese: ‥‥そうですね。 おそらく、サイフ‥‥。
English: Um... I'm pretty sure it was a wallet...

Japanese: 弁護人！　スイソクで しゃべられては困りますな！
English: This is a court of law! You can't just make wild guesses!

Japanese: まったくです。
English: Agreed.

Japanese: ダメですよ！　成歩堂さん！
English: They're right, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ちゃんと法廷記録を見て 答えないと！
English: You have to check the Court Record before you answer!

Japanese: 依頼人さん、ユーザイに なっちゃいますよ！
English: If you don't, your client might end up with a guilty verdict!

Japanese: （依頼人さん、‥‥て、 きみなんだけど‥‥）
English: ("Your client"? You do realize that's you, right?)

Japanese: もう一度、聞きますよ。
English: I will ask you one more time.

Japanese: それはもちろん‥‥ こわれたメガネ、ですね。
English: That's simple, Your Honor. A broken pair of glasses.

Japanese: やはり、警察官だけに 警察手帳、とか‥‥。
English: If he's a cop, then I guess maybe a badge or something...

Japanese: 弁護人！　テキトーなことを 言わんでもらいたい！
English: Mr. Wright! Please stick to the facts of this case!

Japanese: だから、“とか”と 言ったじゃないですか！
English: He is a policeman, correct?

Japanese: そそ、そういう問題じゃない！
English: I don't think you understand the problem here.

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人！ いいかげんな発言は認めません。
English: I will not have an uninformed lawyer in my court.

Japanese: はい‥‥。 （ごまかそうと思ったのに）
English: Yes, Your Honor. (Guess there's no fooling those two.)

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！　ちゃんと 見てください！　法廷記録。
English: Please, for my sake, look at the Court Record, sir!

Japanese: （法廷記録‥‥ Ｒボタンだったかな？）
English: (Uh, it's the Court Record Button, right?)

Japanese: もう一度、聞きますよ。
English: I will ask you one more time.

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおりです。
English: That's right.

Japanese: つき落とされた瞬間、被害者は 犯人のメガネをむしり取ったッス。
English: The victim grabbed the criminal's glasses as he was being shoved, sir,

Japanese: そして、それを握りしめたまま 転落したッス。
English: and held onto them as he fell.

Japanese: なな、なんですか その冷たい視線はッ！
English: Hey! Why are you giving me the evil eye!?

Japanese: きみが今かけてる、 そのメガネは‥‥？
English: Those glasses you're wearing...

Japanese: うううう。
English: Nnnngh...

Japanese: こ、これはたしかに スペアのメガネッス。
English: Yes, this is my spare pair.

Japanese: でも！　現場にあったメガネは アタシのじゃないッス！
English: But these glasses they found at the scene of the crime are not mine! I swear, sir!

Japanese: え。 きみのじゃない‥‥？
English: You sure about that?

Japanese: あたしのは、事件のあった日、 グーゼン踏みつぶしちゃって‥‥。
English: Look, it was a coincidence that on that same day, I accidentally stepped on mine!

Japanese: （グ、グーゼン‥‥）
English: (A "coincidence" she says... Urk...)

Japanese: うふふふふふふふふふふふふふふふ ‥‥裁判長。
English: Eh hee hee hee hee hee hee! Your Honor.

Japanese: 証拠は、メガネだけじゃ ありませんぞ。
English: I have further evidence to present.

Japanese: ほお。‥‥それはすごい。
English: Oh? You have more?

Japanese: さらに決定的な 証拠があるのですよ。
English: And this evidence is very decisive.

Japanese: よろしい！
English: Very well!

Japanese: では、その“証拠”について、 証言してもらいましょう。
English: Let's hear from our witness about this "evidence".

Japanese: ～決定的な証拠～
English: -- Decisive Evidence --

Japanese: 死体の下から見つかったメガネより さらに強力な証拠があるッス。
English: There's something even more incriminating than the glasses under the victim's body, sir.

Japanese: 被害者はデート中、ベンチのあたり から突き落とされたッスが‥‥、
English: During his date, the victim was pushed from the bench area.

Japanese: その際、犯人の名前を地面に 書き残していたッス。
English: But he managed to write the culprit's name on the ground where he landed.

Japanese: ハッキリと被告の名前が “鈴木”と書かれていたッス。
English: I don't like saying it, but it was clearly the defendant's name, "Maggie", sir.

Japanese: この証拠とメガネをあわせて、 彼女を容疑者として逮捕したッス。
English: With this piece of evidence and the glasses, it's hard to say she's not the culprit.

Japanese: 裁判長。これがその写真です。
English: This is a picture of the writing, Your Honor.

Japanese: これは‥‥！
English: Why, this is...!

Japanese: たしかに“スズキ”と 書いてありますね‥‥。
English: Yes, I can see the name is clearly written here.

Japanese: この写真を、証拠として 提出したいと思います。
English: The prosecution would like to submit this picture.

Japanese: わかりました。受理します。
English: Understood. The court accepts it into evidence.

Japanese: 証拠品《現場写真・２》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Crime Photo 2 added to the Court Record.

Japanese: メガネだけでもじゅうぶん アヤシイのに、
English: As if the glasses alone didn't make you look suspicious,

Japanese: 名前まで書き残されてる じゃないか！　被害者に！
English: the victim even wrote your name clear as day on the ground!

Japanese: だだ、だから、あのメガネは アタシのじゃないんです！
English: But, but, but, I already told you! Those glasses aren't mine!!

Japanese: ‥‥じゃあ、 “スズキ”のほうは？
English: And how do you explain his dying message?

Japanese: ワナッス！ これはもう、インボーッス！
English: It's a conspiracy! I'm not guilty, sir!

Japanese: ‥‥では、弁護人。 尋問をおねがいします。
English: Mr. Wright, you may cross- examine the witness.

Japanese: ‥‥じ、じんもん‥‥？
English: Cross-examine?

Japanese: いよいよホンバンッスね！ スズキ、キタイしてるッス！
English: This is it! I'm counting on you!

Japanese: ‥‥と言われても‥‥ 何をすればいいのかな。
English: Sure... But what am I supposed to do?

Japanese: ええええええええええっ！
English: WHAT!?

Japanese: らしくないッス！ いつもの成歩堂さんなら、
English: This isn't like you at all!

Japanese: 今ごろもう、ガンガン証人を しめ上げているはずッス！
English: Normally, this is the part where you get in the witnesses' faces!

Japanese: しめ上げる‥‥って、 どうやって！
English: Get in their faces and do what?

Japanese: しかたないッス！
English: I guess there's no way around it!

Japanese: 成歩堂さんのピンチ！　ここは スズキ、ひとハダ脱ぐッス！
English: OK, I'm going to lend you a hand!

Japanese: 検察側の証人たちはみんな、 何かをかくしていたり、
English: The prosecution's witnesses all hide things from the court,

Japanese: ときにはウソをついていたり するんです！
English: which means they lie from time to time.

Japanese: ウソ‥‥？
English: Lie?

Japanese: でも‥‥あの刑事さん、 上司なんだろ？　きみの。
English: But... isn't that detective your superior?

Japanese: 本人にウソをつく気がなくても、 “思いちがい”ということが。
English: Well, even if they don't mean to lie, sometimes people just remember things wrong.

Japanese: ‥‥ああ、なるほどね。 あの刑事、そそっかしそうだし。
English: Hmm, like that detective. He does sort of look like a scatterbrain...

Japanese: ウソでも思いちがいでも、 こっちには同じコトッス！
English: It doesn't matter! Either way, it's bad for us, sir!

Japanese: とにかく尋問では、 証言のウソを暴くッス！
English: That's why when you question witnesses, you have to find and expose their lies!

Japanese: 弁護人。早く尋問を。 ‥‥おねがいします。
English: Mr. Wright. Your cross-examination, please.

Japanese: は‥‥はい！
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: （‥‥いきなり絶体絶命かよ！）
English: (Talk about trial by fire. Here goes nothing.)

Japanese: （とにかく“ウソを暴け！”が キーワードだな‥‥！）
English: (As long as I can "expose the lies", we should be alright.)

Japanese: ～決定的な証拠～
English: -- Decisive Evidence --

Japanese: 死体の下から見つかったメガネより さらに強力な証拠があるッス。
English: There's something even more incriminating than the glasses under the victim's body, sir.

Japanese: 被害者はデート中、ベンチのあたり から突き落とされたッスが‥‥、
English: During his date, the victim was pushed from the bench area.

Japanese: その際、犯人の名前を地面に 書き残していたッス。
English: But he managed to write the culprit's name on the ground where he landed.

Japanese: ハッキリと被告の名前が “鈴木”と書かれていたッス。
English: I don't like saying it, but it was clearly the defendant's name, "Maggie", sir.

Japanese: この証拠とメガネをあわせて、 彼女を容疑者として逮捕したッス。
English: With this piece of evidence and the glasses, it's hard to say she's not the culprit.

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！　がんばって！ ファイトファイト！
English: Don't give up! Keep that fighting spirit going!

Japanese: そ、そう言われてもなあ‥‥。
English: I'm glad you're all pumped up, but...

Japanese: スズキ、成歩堂さんの トクイ技が見たいッス！
English: I really want to see your "special move", sir!

Japanese: ‥‥な、何それ？ トクイワザ‥‥って。
English: My what??

Japanese: あれッスよ！ 証拠品を《つきつける》ヤツ！
English: You always look so cool when you present evidence!

Japanese: 《つきつける》‥‥ねえ‥‥。
English: Present... evidence?

Japanese: じつはぼくも、そろそろ 見せようと思っていたんだ。
English: Actually, I was just thinking about that!

Japanese: 来たァッ！ 成歩堂さんが帰ってきたッス！
English: Yes! The great Phoenix Wright is back!

Japanese: あ、そうだ！
English: Oh, that's right!

Japanese: なに？
English: Huh?

Japanese: 最近は、つきつけられるのは 証拠品だけじゃなくて、
English: I heard that lately, you can present not only evidence, but people's profiles as well!

Japanese: 関係者のファイルも行けるッス。 ‥‥気をつけたほうがいいッス！
English: It sure makes things a bit more complicated, so be careful, sir!

Japanese: （関係者のファイル‥‥か）
English: (People's profiles, huh?)

Japanese: （よし。もう一度、 証言を聞いてみよう‥‥）
English: (Alright, let's give this another try.)

Japanese: 証拠品を《つきつける》‥‥？
English: Um, about this "presenting evidence"...

Japanese: はいッ！　‥‥証言と法廷記録を、 よーく見くらべるんです。
English: OK. When you're listening to testimony, you can compare it with the Court Record.

Japanese: すると、アキラカにムジュン してることがあるッス！
English: If you do that, you're sure to find contradictions in the witness's statements!

Japanese: ‥‥ムジュン‥‥
English: C-Contradictions?

Japanese: 証人がウソついてたり、 カンちがいしてたり‥‥。
English: Well, there are many reasons why a testimony might contradict the evidence.

Japanese: 理由はいろいろですケド。
English: The witness might be lying, or maybe they're just mistaken.

Japanese: ふんふん。‥‥それで？
English: Uh huh. And?

Japanese: ここまで言って、 まだ思い出さないんですかッ！
English: You still have no idea what I'm talking about??

Japanese: ムジュンした証言を見つけたら、 くいちがう法廷記録のページを‥‥
English: When you find a contradiction, open the Court Record to the item you need...

Japanese: そうか！ 《つきつける》ワケだね！
English: And then I present that evidence, right!?

Japanese: そのとおりッス！
English: You got it!

Japanese: つきつけられるのは、 証拠品だけじゃなくて、
English: You can also present people's profiles as evidence!

Japanese: 関係者のファイルも行けるッス。 ‥‥気をつけたほうがいいッス！
English: With so many items, make sure you present the right thing!

Japanese: へえええ‥‥。やるね。
English: Hmm... Sounds complicated, but I'll give it a try. You're pretty good at this.

Japanese: プロに感心されるなんて！ スズキ、ちょっとフクザツッス！
English: Wow, being praised by a pro! I don't know what to say!

Japanese: ‥‥えーと、 そのメガネですが‥‥
English: Hmm, about those glasses...

Japanese: 被告のものだという、 ハッキリした証拠はあるんですか？
English: Do you have any proof that those belong to my client?

Japanese: レンズは近視用のもので、被告の 視力にだいたい合っていたッス。
English: The lenses are for near- sightedness, and are almost the same strength as hers.

Japanese: フレームの形も、身分証明書の 写真と、おおむね一致したッス。
English: Even the frames look kinda like the ones she's wearing in her ID, pal.

Japanese: ‥‥ふうん。 （どうしよう‥‥）
English: Hmm... (What should I do now?)

Japanese: （‥‥もし、あのメガネが 彼女のものだったら‥‥）
English: (We're in real trouble if those glasses really are hers...)

Japanese: （ここは、ヘタにつっこまない ほうがいいな）
English: (It's probably better to back out while we can.)

Japanese: うう‥‥成歩堂さんが 信じてくれないッス‥‥。
English: Argh... Why don't you believe me...?

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！
English: Hold it!

Japanese: “だいたい”とか“おおむね”とか 証言がアイマイです！
English: "Almost" and "kinda" are not good enough in a case like this!

Japanese: むぐ。
English: Er, um...

Japanese: もっと、ハッキリした 証拠はないんですか！
English: Do you have more definitive proof?

Japanese: メガネと被告をむすぶ、 決定的な証拠です！
English: Is there something that clearly links the defendant with those glasses!?

Japanese: それが‥‥その。
English: Er, um, uh...

Japanese: 砂地とのマサツで、メガネには 指紋その他の痕跡はなかったッス。
English: The dirt and sand rubbed out any traces of fingerprints or anything else.

Japanese: つまり！
English: So what you are saying, detective,

Japanese: このメガネが被告のものだという 証拠は、ないわけですね！
English: is that you have nothing that proves those glasses are my client's.

Japanese: そ、そういうコトッス。
English: Um, something like that...

Japanese: なな、なんですって！
English: Wh-Wh-What!?

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう‥‥証拠はない‥‥。
English: I see... Hmm... So there is no proof...

Japanese: さッすが、成歩堂さん！
English: Wow, that was amazing!

Japanese: スズキ、 モーレツにカンドーッス！
English: I could totally feel it, down in my gut!

Japanese: 突き落とされたのは まちがいないんですか？
English: Now, you're sure he was pushed and that's how he fell?

Japanese: 被害者のカラダに残った キズの状態から、
English: Yeah, pal. If you look at the wounds on the victim's body,

Japanese: まず、まちがいないと 考えられるッス。
English: there's no way it was anything else.

Japanese: ううん‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: では、証言をつづけてください。
English: Please continue with your testimony, detective.

Japanese: とにかく、被害者は 突き落とされたッスが‥‥、
English: Anyway, the victim fell pretty far...

Japanese: 名前‥‥ですか？
English: The culprit's name?

Japanese: そッス。‥‥自分も、 ショックだったッス。
English: Yeah. I was surprised, too.

Japanese: できれば、 信じたくなかった‥‥
English: I didn't want to believe it, but...

Japanese: “スズキ　マコ”って 書いてあったんですか？
English: Was the name that of my client?

Japanese: まちがいありませんか！
English: Are you absolutely certain!?

Japanese: ザンネンながら、 まちがいないッス。
English: Sorry, pal, but that's what it said.

Japanese: これが、その写真ッス。 ‥‥ダレが見たってスズキッス。
English: This is a picture of it. No No matter which way you look, it still says "Maggie".

Japanese: （ううん‥‥たしかに‥‥）
English: (Hmm... He's got a point...)

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと、成歩堂さん！
English: Hey, hold on!

Japanese: え？
English: Huh?

Japanese: “え”じゃないッス！ たしかにあれ、スズキッスけど‥‥
English: Don't "huh" me! I know the picture says "Maggie", but...

Japanese: （‥‥言われてみれば‥‥。 なんか、おかしいような‥‥）
English: (Now that she mentions it, something does feel kind of off about this picture...)

Japanese: それが“ムジュン”ッス！ ‥‥さあ、《つきつける》ッス！
English: That's how you know you found a contradiction! Now hurry up and present some evidence!

Japanese: （ムジュン‥‥か‥‥）
English: (So THAT'S what spotting a contradiction feels like...)

Japanese: （とりあえず、法廷記録を 見てみようかな‥‥）
English: (I'd better check the Court Record again...)

Japanese: その“スズキ”という文字‥‥。 まちがいなく、被害者が？
English: And you are certain that it was the victim who wrote the name on the ground?

Japanese: 被害者の指には、砂による スリキズがあったッス。
English: There were scratches on his fingers from the rough sand,

Japanese: また、人さし指のツメの間から 砂粒も検出されているッス。
English: and there were grains of sand stuck under his pointer finger nail.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。どうやら、被害者の 指で書かれたようですね。
English: Hmm... It certainly seems that the name was written by the victim himself.

Japanese: （まいったな‥‥）
English: (That didn't go well.)

Japanese: （ホントに被害者が書いたのなら、 ヤッカイだぞ‥‥）
English: (If it really was him, then we're in a lot of trouble...)

Japanese: ど‥‥どうかしましたか？
English: Wh-What is it?

Japanese: （なんだ、この感じ‥‥）
English: (What... What's come over me...?)

Japanese: （気がついたら思わず叫んでいた！ ‥‥“異議あり”って‥‥）
English: (Without thinking, I just blurted out, "Objection!"...)

Japanese: （それもハラの底から、大声で。 人さし指までつきつけて！）
English: (And I yelled it at the top of my lungs, finger outstretched, ready to take on my opponent!)

Japanese: （感じるぞ‥‥体中にチカラが みちていく！）
English: (What a rush!)

Japanese: ‥‥証人ッ！
English: Detective Gumshoe!

Japanese: なな‥‥な、なんスか？
English: Y-You talking to me, pal?

Japanese: 被告人の名前を 言ってみてください！
English: Please state the defendant's name for me!

Japanese: そんなこと‥‥ なんの意味もありませんぞ！
English: What are you trying to prove with this futile exercise, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: とんでもない！　非常に重要な 意味があります。
English: You'll see. This is a very crucial line of questioning!

Japanese: じゃあ、検事さんでもいい。 ‥‥被告の名前を。
English: Actually, Mr. Payne, you can answer. The defendant's name, if you please.

Japanese: なな、なんなんですか その妙なハクリョクは‥‥。
English: Wh-Where is this ridiculous question coming from?

Japanese: ひ、被告の名前ですか？ ‥‥スズキ　マコ、でしょう？
English: The defendant's, uh, name is, uh... "Maggie Byrde".

Japanese: カタカナではなく、 漢字でおねがいします。
English: I think someone needs to check the Court Record.

Japanese: かか、漢字‥‥？ それはもちろん、『須々木　マコ』
English: What...? It says right here that it's "Maggey Byrde".

Japanese: いいッ！！
English: Aaaah!!

Japanese: ‥‥おわかり いただけたようですね。
English: It looks like the bird caught the cat napping!

Japanese: ど、どういうことですか！
English: What's going on here!?

Japanese: ゼンゼンわからねッス！
English: I have no idea either, sir!

Japanese: いいですか！
English: As you can see,

Japanese: 被害者が書き残した名前は、 漢字で“鈴木”と書かれていた。
English: the victim did indeed leave a name, "Maggie".

Japanese: しかし、被告の名前は “須々木　マコ”です。
English: However, the defendant's name is actually spelled, "Maggey"!

Japanese: これはあきらかに、 ムジュンしています！
English: This is a blatant contradiction of facts!

Japanese: ああああっ！
English: Ohh!

Japanese: ゼンゼン気がつかなかったッス！
English: How about that? I hadn't even noticed!

Japanese: し、しかし‥‥！
English: But, but, but...!

Japanese: それはたまたま、被害者が 正しい名前を知らなかった‥‥
English: But maybe the victim didn't know how to spell her name correctly...

Japanese: 検事はさきほど、こう言いました。
English: May I remind you that it was you who said,

Japanese: 『被告人は、恋人を殺害した 罪に問われている』‥‥。
English: "The defendant is accused of killing her lover."

Japanese: コイビトの名前を 知らないはずがない！
English: If they were truly lovers, it would be impossible for him to have not known her name!

Japanese: むぐぐゥッ！
English: Noooo!

Japanese: たしかに、そのとおりです。
English: This is very true.

Japanese: 亜内検事！
English: Mr. Payne.

Japanese: は‥‥はっ？
English: Y-Yes, Your Honor?

Japanese: そもそも‥‥被告人と 被害者の町尾　守くんは、
English: Are you absolutely certain that the defendant and the victim, Dustin Prince,

Japanese: 本当に恋愛関係にあったのですか？
English: were, in fact, lovers?

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥。そのはずです。
English: Y-Yes, I am quite certain, Your Honor.

Japanese: 警察署内でも、有名なカップル だったそうで‥‥。
English: They were a well-known couple in the police force.

Japanese: では、イトノコギリ刑事。
English: Detective Gumshoe.

Japanese: 証言してください。 被告人と被害者の関係について。
English: Please testify for the court the relationship between the victim and the defendant.

Japanese: 了解ッス‥‥。
English: Yes, sir...

Japanese: ～被害者と被告人の関係～
English: -- Dustin and Maggey --

Japanese: 町尾巡査とマコ巡査は、半年ほど つきあっているはずッス。
English: Officer Prince and Officer Byrde had been going out for about half a year.

Japanese: “ケッコン”の話も、ボチボチ 出ていたみたいッス‥‥。
English: It sounded like they were even talking about marriage.

Japanese: 事件があった日は、被害者の 誕生日だったッス。
English: The day of the incident just happened to be the victim's birthday, sir.

Japanese: マコクン‥‥マコ巡査は、 プレゼントを用意して‥‥。
English: Maggey... I mean, Officer Byrde, had gotten Officer Prince a present.

Japanese: そのプレゼントは、２ヶ月も前から ジュンビしていたッス。
English: It was something she had gotten over 2 months ago.

Japanese: ‥‥自分が相談を受けたから 知ってるッス。
English: I should know, 'cause she came to me to ask what she should get for him.

Japanese: ほおほお、２人の仲はそんなに‥‥
English: Oh... Those two sound like they were close...

Japanese: だからこそ、 ヒゲキが起こったのですよ。
English: Nevertheless, tragedy struck.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥なるほど。 では弁護人、尋問を。
English: Hmm, yes, I see... You may cross-examine the witness, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ～被害者と被告人の関係～
English: -- Dustin and Maggey --

Japanese: 町尾巡査とマコ巡査は、半年ほど つきあっているはずッス。
English: Officer Prince and Officer Byrde had been going out for about half a year.

Japanese: “ケッコン”の話も、ボチボチ 出ていたみたいッス‥‥。
English: It sounded like they were even talking about marriage.

Japanese: 事件があった日は、被害者の 誕生日だったッス。
English: The day of the incident just happened to be the victim's birthday, sir.

Japanese: マコクン‥‥マコ巡査は、 プレゼントを用意して‥‥。
English: Maggey... I mean, Officer Byrde, had gotten Officer Prince a present.

Japanese: そのプレゼントは、２ヶ月も前から ジュンビしていたッス。
English: It was something she had gotten over 2 months ago.

Japanese: ‥‥自分が相談を受けたから 知ってるッス。
English: I should know, 'cause she came to me to ask what she should get for him.

Japanese: こ、今度はどうしました？
English: Wh-What is it this time?

Japanese: ‥‥今の証言に、 《ムジュン》なんてあるかな？
English: That testimony didn't sound like it had any contradictions in it to me.

Japanese: たいしたこと しゃべってないし‥‥。
English: There just wasn't anything that really stuck out as odd.

Japanese: そうですね‥‥。
English: Yeah...

Japanese: もっと情報を引き出して みたらどうですか？
English: I wonder what would happen if you tried to get more information from him?

Japanese: 情報を‥‥？
English: Get more information?

Japanese: 思いっきり《ゆさぶる》！ お得意のアレッス！
English: Yeah! You know! Like how they "press" people on those old cop shows, sir!

Japanese: 《ゆさぶる》ねえ‥‥。
English: So I should try "pressing" him, huh?

Japanese: なんでそんなことを 知っているんですか？
English: How do you know about this?

Japanese: 今年の３月に、署内で研修旅行が あったッス。
English: Every year in March, we have a training camp for us cops.

Japanese: 新人のマコクンと町尾巡査は、 そこでねんごろに。
English: Officer Byrde was a rookie at the time, and she and Officer Prince seemed to hit it off.

Japanese: （ねんごろ‥‥ねえ）
English: (They got close, I take it...)

Japanese: ホントは自分も参加するはず だったッスが‥‥
English: Actually, I was supposed to go, too, but...

Japanese: 旅行の積立金が払えなくて 行けなかったッス。
English: I couldn't pay the deposit for the trip, so I didn't.

Japanese: 行ってさえいれば、今ごろは‥‥
English: If only I had gone on that trip...

Japanese: なんですか？
English: What is it?

Japanese: い、いや。なんでもないッス。 とにかく‥‥、
English: Oh, uh, nothing, sir! Really! Anyway...

Japanese: ケッコン‥‥って、トシが ８つもはなれてますね‥‥。
English: Marriage? But wasn't the victim 8 years older than her?

Japanese: なんスか！　ケッコン相手は 同級生から探せって言うッスか！
English: What!? You saying a guy's gotta marry someone the same age as himself, pal!?

Japanese: い、いえいえ。 ‥‥そういうワケでは‥‥
English: No, that's not what I meant at all...

Japanese: イトノコさんと町尾さん、 １つちがいなんですよ。
English: Detective Gumshoe and Dustin were only a year apart, you know?

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥。あのようすじゃ、 ケッコンはまだみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Ugh... I think this fella has a ways to go before marriage...)

Japanese: よけいなおせわッス！
English: Mind your own business, pal!

Japanese: 事件があった日、というと‥‥ ９月６日ですか？
English: The day of the incident... You mean September 6th?

Japanese: そッス。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: 被害者の町尾巡査は、５時半で 勤務が終わりだったッスが、
English: The victim, Officer Prince, had just gotten off duty at 5:30 PM that day,

Japanese: マコクンは夜勤だったので、 ２人は公園でヒトトキの‥‥
English: and since Maggey's night shift hadn't started yet, they went to the park for a bit...

Japanese: アイビキを楽しんだ、と。 いいですねえ、ワカい人たちは。
English: Ah, I remember when I was young and in love. Oh, it was a jolly time.

Japanese: （なんでもいいけど、 ノンキなじいさんだな‥‥）
English: (That's great, Your Honor... I'm glad you're such a cheerful old man...)

Japanese: ‥‥被告のこと、ずいぶん くわしいんですね。
English: ... You seem to know a lot about the defendant.

Japanese: ま、そ、そりゃ上司ッスから。 部下をカントクするギムが‥‥
English: Well, that's because, uh, I'm her boss. And I've gotta watch out for my subordinates!

Japanese: デートのプレゼントまで？ ちょっとカントクしすぎでは‥‥
English: But even what she was going to give as a present? Isn't that going a bit too far...?

Japanese: じゃあなんスか！ アンタはこう言うッスか！
English: Hey, pal! Watch what you say!

Japanese: 部下がまんびきしても、 知らんプリしろと！
English: I know everything that happens under me! If someone so much as scratches their...

Japanese: い、いやべつにその‥‥
English: I REALLY don't need to know that much...

Japanese: 弁護人！　ここは、証人を いじめる場所ではありませんぞ！
English: Mr. Wright! Please refrain from badgering the witness!

Japanese: そうですね。
English: I agree.

Japanese: この証人が、被告のおシリを 追っかけまわしていたとしても、
English: Even if this witness has a crush on the defendant,

Japanese: それは、今、 問題にすべきコトではありません。
English: that should not be the point of discussion at this time.

Japanese: ぎゃー。なんてコト 言うッスかぁッ！
English: Whoa! Wait a second! Why are we talking about this!?

Japanese: ア、アンタのせいッス！ 逮捕ッス！　もー有罪ッス！
English: It's all YOUR fault, pal! You're guilty, guilty, guilty! I should have you arrested!

Japanese: （‥‥どうも、あの刑事とは うまくやっていけそうもないな）
English: (I think the good Detective is about done here...)

Japanese: “２ヶ月も前から”‥‥？
English: "Over 2 months ago?"

Japanese: そッス。 ‥‥けなげな女の子ッス。
English: Yup. She's a very considerate woman, pal!

Japanese: それで‥‥ 誕生プレゼントには、何を？
English: So... What was this birthday present?

Japanese: グローブッス。
English: She got him a glove.

Japanese: “グローブッス”‥‥ですか？
English: A single glove? Why would she only give him one?

Japanese: いやいや。グローブですグローブ。 野球の。
English: Um, actually, Your Honor, the glove in question is a baseball glove.

Japanese: ああ、はいはい。 グラブね。ベースボールの。
English: Oh, I see. A baseball glove.

Japanese: 被害者の町尾巡査は、 野球が大好きだったッス‥‥。
English: Officer Prince was a huge baseball fan.

Japanese: （グローブ‥‥ねえ‥‥。 どうなんだろう？）
English: (A baseball glove. Hmm...)

Japanese: （まあ、誕生プレゼントなんて どうでもいいかな‥‥）
English: (Well, it's just a birthday present...)

Japanese: 質問はないようですね。
English: If there are no further questions, Your Honor...

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 証人。あなたはなぜ、 そのグラブのことを‥‥？
English: Witness, why do you know about this glove?

Japanese: ところでさっき、 証人はこう言いましたね。
English: Just now, I believe you said that the present was something

Japanese: “プレゼントを、２ヶ月も前から ジュンビしていた”‥‥と。
English: she had "gotten over 2 months ago".

Japanese: 言ったッス。
English: Yeah.

Japanese: グローブを買うのに、２ヶ月も かかったんですか？
English: Are you saying she went out and bought the glove over 2 months ago?

Japanese: そんなワケないッス！
English: Nah, nothing like that, pal!

Japanese: じゃあ、 どんなワケだったんですか？
English: Then, what is it like?

Japanese: モチロン、作らせたッス。 ‥‥特注で。
English: She ordered it. It was custom-made!

Japanese: ‥‥特注？　グローブを？
English: Custom-made? The glove was custom-made?

Japanese: そッス。グローブッス。
English: Yup, that's what I said!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。 グローブッスをねえ‥‥
English: Hmm... So the glove was custom-made.

Japanese: 誕生プレゼントなど、 事件とは関係ありませんぞ。
English: Your Honor, I really don't see how this glove is related to this case.

Japanese: たしかに、関係なさそうですねえ。 ‥‥どうなんですか、弁護人。
English: Yes, it would seem that there is little relevance. What do you think, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: その特注のグローブとやらが、 何か関係あるのですかな？
English: Do you think this glove is really relevant to this case?

Japanese: ないと思いますよ、モチロン。
English: Hmm, I suppose it isn't really relevant.

Japanese: なな、なんですとォ！
English: Th-Then...

Japanese: 関係ないなら、 こんなに引っぱらないように！
English: Then why were you wasting this court's time with irrelevant questions!?

Japanese: す、すみません。 ちょっと興味があったもので‥‥。
English: Uh, I was curious and got, uh, carried away, Your Honor...?

Japanese: 証人。こんな弁護人はほっといて、 証言をつづけるように。
English: Witness, please ignore this airheaded lawyer and continue with your testimony.

Japanese: じゃあ、証人。あなたはなぜ、 グローブのことを‥‥？
English: Detective Gumshoe, please tell the court why you knew about the baseball glove.

Japanese: （‥‥そんなの ぼくにもわからないけど‥‥）
English: (I don't know where this will lead me, but...)

Japanese: モチロン関係があります！
English: Of course it is relevant!

Japanese: そのグローブこそが、この事件の カギをにぎっているのです！
English: That glove is the key to this whole case!

Japanese: 出たァッ！ なんのコンキョもないハッタリ！
English: Yes! Bluffing to the max!

Japanese: やっと成歩堂さんらしく なってきましたね！
English: Now, THIS is the Mr. Wright I know!

Japanese: スズキ、うれしいッス！ シビれるッス！　カンゲキッス！
English: I'm so happy you're back, sir! I was wondering how long it'd take! This is great!

Japanese: （《ゆさぶる》か‥‥。 そういえば、こうやって）
English: (Hmm, pressing people... It feels like I've done this before.)

Japanese: （ビシバシ人にツッコミまくって いたような気もするなあ‥‥）
English: (As if I used to do this to squeeze information from even the most tight-lipped people.)

Japanese: わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: そこまで言うなら、もう少し 話を聞いてみましょう！
English: If you are that convinced, then let's hear some more about this matter.

Japanese: 被告人から、 プレゼントの相談を？
English: You discussed what the defendant was going to give to her boyfriend?

Japanese: まあ、自分はソノ‥‥おほん。 信頼‥‥されているッスから。
English: Well, I'm, er... *ahem* She, uh... trusts me, so...

Japanese: （しあわせそうに モジモジしてるぞ‥‥）
English: (Boy does he look proud of himself right now...)

Japanese: じつは、持ってきてるッス。 そのグローブ。
English: Actually, I brought the glove with me today.

Japanese: え。
English: And?

Japanese: それなら話が早い。 さっそく、提出してください。
English: Why didn't you say so earlier? Hurry and show the glove to this court!

Japanese: 事件とは関係ないと 思ったものッスから‥‥。
English: Well, I didn't think it had anything to do with this case...

Japanese: これッス。
English: Anyway, this is it, sir.

Japanese: ‥‥また、ずいぶんハデに 黄色いグラブですねえ‥‥。
English: It's, uh... rather yellow, isn't it?

Japanese: 証拠品《グローブ》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Baseball Glove added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 被害者の町尾巡査は、 それは黄色が好きだったッス。
English: Officer Prince really liked the color yellow.

Japanese: ‥‥だから、わざわざ特注で？
English: And that's why you had to special order it?

Japanese: ええ、そうなんです！ それに、もう１つ‥‥
English: Yup, that's right! That, and one other reason...

Japanese: ‥‥そこまで。 これでハッキリしました。
English: I think this court has heard enough.

Japanese: 被害者と被告は、やはり 恋愛関係にあったようですね。
English: It is clear that the victim and the defendant were involved with each other.

Japanese: そのとおりです。
English: Yes, that's correct, Your Honor.

Japanese: しかし、そうなると、 気になることがあります。
English: Now, if that is true, it brings up an important question.

Japanese: この“鈴木”という文字‥‥。 本当に被害者が書いたものなのか？
English: Was the name "Maggie" really written by the victim?

Japanese: かしこまりました。
English: I see your point, Your Honor.

Japanese: その文字について、くわしく 証言してもらいましょうか。
English: Detective Gumshoe, please tell the court a little more about the name on the ground.

Japanese: ‥‥了解ッス。
English: Yes, sir.

Japanese: ～被害者が残した文字について～
English: -- Writing on the Ground --

Japanese: ワレワレはまず、残された文字の 筆跡を調べてみたッス。
English: We first looked into the handwriting, sir.

Japanese: ザンネンながら、被害者が書いたと 断定はできなかったッス。
English: Unfortunately, we couldn't confirm that it was the victim's handwriting.

Japanese: 次にワレワレは、 被害者の人さし指を調べたッス。
English: Next, we checked the victim's pointer finger.

Japanese: ツメの間に、砂のツブがはさまって いるのが発見されたッス。
English: We found that there was sand trapped under the victim's fingernail.

Japanese: 人さし指のハラにも、 文字を書いた際のスリ傷が。
English: There were also scratches on the skin that were caused by him writing on the ground.

Japanese: 以上のことから、被害者は右手で あの字を書いたと断定できるッス！
English: From this, we could confirm that the victim wrote this name with his right hand.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥たしかに、 そう考えるのが自然でしょうね。
English: Hmm... Yes, a perfectly logical conclusion.

Japanese: では、弁護人。 尋問をおねがいします。
English: Now then, Mr. Wright, you may cross-examine the witness.

Japanese: ‥‥はい。
English: Thank you, Your Honor.

Japanese: ～被害者が残した文字について～
English: -- Writing on the Ground --

Japanese: ワレワレはまず、残された文字の 筆跡を調べてみたッス。
English: We first looked into the handwriting.

Japanese: ザンネンながら、被害者が書いたと 断定はできなかったッス。
English: Unfortunately, we couldn't confirm that it was the victim's handwriting.

Japanese: 次にワレワレは、 被害者の人さし指を調べたッス。
English: Next, we checked the victim's pointer finger.

Japanese: ツメの間に、砂のツブがはさまって いるのが発見されたッス。
English: We found that there was sand trapped under the victim's fingernail.

Japanese: 人さし指のハラにも、 文字を書いた際のスリ傷が。
English: There were also scratches on the skin that were caused by him writing on the ground.

Japanese: 以上のことから、被害者は右手で あの字を書いたと断定できるッス！
English: From this, we could confirm that the victim wrote this name with his right hand.

Japanese: （こうして、 証言だけ聞いていると‥‥）
English: (Listening to this, you would think there was only one conclusion...)

Japanese: （あの文字、被害者が 書いたとしか思えないな‥‥）
English: (that the name was definitely written by the victim...)

Japanese: でも、それは どう考えてもヘンッス。
English: But don't you think that would be really strange, sir!?

Japanese: ‥‥もし町尾さんが右手で あの文字を書けたのなら、
English: If Dustin really wrote that message with his right hand,

Japanese: プレゼントを用意するのに、 あんなに苦労しなかったッス！
English: do you think I would have gone through that much trouble to get him his present?

Japanese: （‥‥プレゼント‥‥？）
English: (The present...? What about it...?)

Japanese: ‥‥でも、砂に書かれた文字で 筆跡まで特定できるんですか？
English: But can you really determine handwriting based on a sample written in sand?

Japanese: ハッ！　シロートは これだからイヤッスね！
English: Heh, this is why amateurs are amateurs.

Japanese: ワレワレをナメてもらっちゃ 困るッス。
English: We're not a buncha simpletons, pal!

Japanese: この国の科学捜査は、国際的に 見ても、まあなかなかのものッス。
English: Scientific investigation in this country's actually pretty good.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。 ビミョーな言い回しですねえ。
English: Hmm, I believe it's time to get back to the real point.

Japanese: ‥‥それで？　ケッキョク、 筆跡を調べた結果は？
English: Agreed, Your Honor. So, what was the result of the investigation?

Japanese: ‥‥なんだ‥‥。 ケッキョクできなかったんですか。
English: So in the end, you couldn't confirm it?

Japanese: なんだとはなんスかッ！
English: Hey, don't you look down on us!

Japanese: ワレワレをナメてもらっちゃ 困るッス！
English: I told you! We're not a bunch of simpletons, pal!

Japanese: できないモノはムリ。 ‥‥それが科学捜査のキホンッス！
English: Everyone knows you can't find out everything you want with scientific investigation!

Japanese: はじめて聞きました。
English: I've never heard that before.

Japanese: 私も。
English: Me, either.

Japanese: ぼくも。
English: Nor I.

Japanese: 警察学校じゃ習わなかったッス。
English: I never heard anything like that at the police academy, sir...

Japanese: ‥‥自分の持論ッス。 とにかく‥‥。
English: OK, so I made it up. Anyway...

Japanese: 人さし指‥‥？
English: His pointer finger?

Japanese: そッス。アンタがいつも ヒトにつきつけてるヤツッス。
English: You know, the one you're always POINTING and waving around in people's faces.

Japanese: はあ‥‥。 気になりますか？　もしかして。
English: Aha, ha, ha. Don't tell me it bothers you...

Japanese: シンゾウに悪いッス。
English: Every time you do it, I have a mini-heart attack. It's like you're trying to kill me, pal.

Japanese: とにかく、被害者の右の人さし指を 調べたわけですね？
English: In any case, you examined the victim's index finger, correct?

Japanese: ユビで砂地に文字を書いたら、 その痕跡が残るはずッスから。
English: Yeah. We figured there should be something on his finger if he had been writing in sand.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥なるほど。 それで、検査の結果は？
English: Hmm... And the results?

Japanese: それは、どういうことですか？
English: And what does that prove?

Japanese: つまり、その指で、ジッサイに 文字を書いたということッス。
English: Well, it proves that he did write that name with his own finger.

Japanese: そうですね。だから、 ツメの間から砂ツブが‥‥。
English: Yes, which explains why there was sand stuck under his nail.

Japanese: （‥‥なるほどね‥‥）
English: (I guess he's right...)

Japanese: まだあるッス。
English: And there's more...

Japanese: スリ傷‥‥ですか？
English: "Scratches on his skin?"

Japanese: そッス。ま、モチロン 肉眼では見えない、小さな傷ッス。
English: Yup. You can't see them with your naked eye, but they're there.

Japanese: はあ‥‥。 スゴいですねそれは。
English: That is incredible!

Japanese: そッス。スゴいッス。 科学捜査ッス！
English: Sure is! That's the power of scientific investigation!

Japanese: 虫メガネのスゴいヤツ ‥‥ええと‥‥
English: They're so small that we had to use a magnifying glass... like a really strong one.

Japanese: なんかこう、科学っぽい名前の アレを使って‥‥
English: It's got that really scientific-sounding name...

Japanese: けんび鏡、ですか？
English: You mean a microscope?

Japanese: そう！　それ！ それで見つけたッス！
English: Yeah, that's it! We used one of those and that's how we found them!

Japanese: （すごくうれしそうだな‥‥）
English: (I can't believe this guy doesn't know what a microscope is...)

Japanese: まちがいありませんか？
English: Are you absolutely sure?

Japanese: 科学のチカラを信じるッス。
English: I believe in the power of science.

Japanese: （ううん‥‥これで証拠は じゅうぶん、なのかな‥‥？）
English: (Hmm, I wonder if my evidence is solid enough to counter with?)

Japanese: ‥‥証人。 これを見てください。
English: Detective Gumshoe. Take a look at this.

Japanese: それは‥‥グローブ、ッスね。
English: That's the glove, right?

Japanese: ‥‥コイツの特徴を 言ってもらえますか？
English: Could you tell the court what is special about this glove?

Japanese: そんなの、 考えるまでもないッス。
English: What's special? Um, never really thought about it, but uh...

Japanese: “ミョーに黄色い” ‥‥これに尽きるッス！
English: It's REALLY yellow... And that's about it.

Japanese: ‥‥そう、たしかに黄色い。 まず、それが１点。
English: Yes, it's REALLY yellow, but that is only one of its qualities.
Huh?

Japanese: じつは、もう１点あるんですよ。
English: There's another reason why it's special.

Japanese: な、なんですか、それは‥‥？
English: And what would that be?

Japanese: カンタンなことです。
English: It's very simple.

Japanese: 左きき用なんですよ。 ‥‥このグローブは。
English: This glove is made for a left-handed person!

Japanese: 左きき‥‥？
English: Left-handed...?

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。たしかに。
English: Why, you're absolutely right!

Japanese: これは、右手にはめるように できているグラブですね。
English: This glove is made to be worn on the right hand!

Japanese: だから、特注だったんですよ。
English: That is why it had to be custom-made.

Japanese: ここまで黄色い、しかも左きき用の グローブなんて、売ってません。
English: I have never seen a bright yellow left-hander's glove for sale. Have you?

Japanese: ま‥‥まあ、たしかに。
English: Well, um... no.

Japanese: ところで、証人。
English: So, Detective.

Japanese: 被害者は、どっちの手で 文字を書いたんでしたっけ‥‥？
English: Which hand did the victim use to write the name with, again...?

Japanese: そんなの、決まってるッス！
English: That's easy!

Japanese: 写真を見れば、アキラカ‥‥ ‥‥あ、アレ‥‥？
English: Look, it's obvious from this picture that it was his... W-W-Wait a sec...

Japanese: お忘れなく。被害者は、 左ききだったんですよ？
English: Don't forget that the victim was left-handed!

Japanese: あ‥‥ああああッ！
English: Aaaaah!!

Japanese: こ‥‥これは、その‥‥。 と、とにかくアレです。異議を‥‥
English: This is... That is... I mean... I... Objec--

Japanese: 却下します。
English: Overruled.

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。つまり‥‥、 どういうことになるんですか？
English: Mr. Wright, I would like to know what your line of reasoning proves.

Japanese: 結論は１つです！
English: There is only one conclusion that can be drawn!

Japanese: 左ききの人間が、右手で メッセージを残すはずがない！
English: A left-handed person could not have written a message with his right hand!

Japanese: つまり！
English: Therefore!

Japanese: “鈴木”と書き残したのは、 被害者ではなかったのです！
English: The person who wrote the name "Maggie" could not have been the victim!

Japanese: せ‥‥静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: ‥‥た、たしかに、 そういうことになります！
English: When you think about it that way, then yes,

Japanese: この文字は、被害者が 書いたものではなかった‥‥！
English: it is not possible that this name was written by the victim himself.

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあマコくんは‥‥！
English: Then that means Maggey is...!

Japanese: そんな‥‥そんなバカなッ！
English: No... IT'S NOT POSSIBLE!!

Japanese: 亜内検事！
English: Mr. Payne.

Japanese: はは‥‥は‥‥はははっ！
English: Y-Y-Yes, Your Honor?

Japanese: 検察側の証拠は、被告の有罪を 立証していません！
English: The evidence the prosecution has presented has failed to prove the defendant's guilt.

Japanese: それどころか、無実を 証明しているではないですか！
English: In fact, I believe you have proven her to be innocent!

Japanese: うぐわァァァッ！
English: NOOOOOOO!

Japanese: やったァ！ 成歩堂さん、サイコー！
English: Alright! You did it, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: スズキ、もーホレまくりッス！
English: Whew! I feel like I can breathe again!

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、 結論は出たようです。
English: It seems that we have reached the conclusion.

Japanese: また１つ、お手がらですね、 成歩堂くん。
English: You did a fine job once again, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: え‥‥？　ぼくですか？ はあ。それはどうも‥‥。
English: Me, Your Honor? Ah, well, thank you, sir...

Japanese: またホメられちゃいましたね！
English: See, you got complimented by the judge again! You're really good!

Japanese: だからヤメられないんですよね！ ‥‥弁護士。
English: And that's why you can't give up being a lawyer, sir!

Japanese: （ジョーダンじゃない！ もう、コリゴリだよ‥‥）
English: (Are you joking!? I'm more than ready to retire!)

Japanese: では、判決を 言いわたしたいと思います。
English: I will now announce my verdict.

Japanese: 被告人・須々木　マコに‥‥
English: This court finds the defendant, Maggey Byrde...

Japanese: ちょっと待っていただきたいッ！
English: No!! Not yet!

Japanese: ‥‥お待ちください、裁判長。
English: I mean, please give me a few more minutes, Your Honor.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですか、亜内検事？
English: Wh-What is the meaning of this, Mr. Payne!?

Japanese: ‥‥検察側の立証は、 まだ終了しておりません！
English: The prosecution is not finished yet!

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！
English: What do you mean!?

Japanese: 検察側は、次の証人を 召喚したいと思います！
English: We would like to call our next witness to the stand!

Japanese: えええぇぇぇっ！
English: Whaaaaaaaaaat!?

Japanese: ‥‥いったい、その証人は 何を目撃したのですか？
English: And what did this witness... witness?

Japanese: 被害者が突き落とされた瞬間を。
English: The moment the victim was pushed to his death!

Japanese: さらに、突き落とした犯人のカオも ハッキリ見ておるのです！
English: What's more, he saw the very face of the culprit!

Japanese: な、なんですって！
English: What the heck!?

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order in the court!

Japanese: では、ここで一度、 休憩をとりたいと思います。
English: I believe a recess is in order.

Japanese: その後、その証人の 証言を聞くとしましょう。
English: Afterward, we will hear from this new witness.

Japanese: （‥‥このまま終わろうなんて、 やっぱりアマかったか‥‥）
English: (I had a feeling that was a bit too easy...)

Japanese: （この休憩時間で、集めるんだ！ 少しでも多く、情報を‥‥）
English: (Hmm, I need more information. I'll have to see what I can find out during this recess.)

Japanese: （本当の勝負は、これからだ！）
English: (I can't let my guard down! It's only going to get tougher from here!)

Japanese: では、これより休憩に入ります！
English: Court is adjourned for recess!

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: This is a clear-cut case with little reason for doubt,

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: and further deliberation will not be required here.

Japanese: 被告人、スズキ　マコに たった今、判決を言いわたします！
English: This court finds the defendant, Maggey Byrde...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. This court is adjourned!
